types.name: AsyncTask
types.summary: This is the base class for async tasks, that are running on Dynamo Scheduler.

constructors.name: AsyncTask(Dynamo.Scheduler.IScheduler)
constructors.summary: Constructs an instance of AsyncTask object.

params.name: scheduler
params.doc: A reference to the DynamoScheduler, this parameter cannot be null.

properties.name: Priority
properties.summary: DynamoScheduler sorts tasks based on two key factors: the priority of a task, and the relative importance between two tasks that have the same priority. During task re-prioritizing process, DynamoScheduler first sorts the tasks in accordance to their AsyncTask.Priority property. The resulting ordered list is then sorted again by calling AsyncTask.Compare method to determine the relative importance among tasks having the same priority.

events.name: Completed
events.summary: This event is raised when the AsyncTask is completed, in the context of ISchedulerThread, any UI element access that is needed should be dispatched onto the UI dispatcher.

types.name: AsyncTaskExtensions
types.summary: Tools for working productively with AsyncTask's

methods.name: AllComplete(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Dynamo.Scheduler.AsyncTask>, System.Action<System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<Dynamo.Scheduler.AsyncTask>>)
methods.summary: Await the completion of a collection of scheduled tasks. The given action will only be executed after all events are complete or discarded. The tasks must already be scheduled and not yet completed or this action will never be executed.

returns.doc: An IDisposable representing all of the event subscription

methods.name: Then(Dynamo.Scheduler.AsyncTask, Dynamo.Scheduler.AsyncTaskCompletedHandler)
methods.summary: Upon completion of the task, invoke the specified action

returns.doc: An IDisposable representing the event subscription

methods.signature: System.IDisposable ThenPost(Dynamo.Scheduler.AsyncTask task, Dynamo.Scheduler.AsyncTaskCompletedHandler action, System.Threading.SynchronizationContext context)
methods.summary: Upon completion of the task, invoke the action asynchronously in the specified SynchronizationContext

returns.doc: An IDisposable representing the event subscription

methods.name: ThenSend(Dynamo.Scheduler.AsyncTask, Dynamo.Scheduler.AsyncTaskCompletedHandler, System.Threading.SynchronizationContext)

methods.summary: Upon completion of the task, invoke the action synchronously in the specified SynchronizationContext

returns.doc: An IDisposable representing the event subscription

types.name: IScheduler
types.summary: This interface provides methods and properties used for Dynamo Scheduler.

methods.name: ProcessNextTask(bool)
methods.summary: An ISchedulerThread implementation calls this method so scheduler starts to process the next task in the queue, if there is any. Note that this method is meant to process only one task in queue. The implementation of ISchedulerThread is free to call this method again in a fashion that matches its task fetching behavior.

params.name: waitIfTaskQueueIsEmpty
params.doc: This parameter is only used if the task queue is empty at the time this method is invoked. When the task queue becomes empty, setting this to true will cause this call to block until either the next task becomes available, or when the scheduler is requested to shutdown.

returns.doc: This method returns true if the task queue is not empty, or false otherwise. Note that this method returns false when scheduler begins to shutdown, even when the task queue is not empty.

methods.name: ScheduleForExecution(Dynamo.Scheduler.AsyncTask)
methods.summary: Callers of this method create an instance of AsyncTask derived class and call this method to schedule the task for execution.

params.name: asyncTask
params.doc: The task to execute asynchronously.

properties.name: NextTimeStamp
properties.summary: AsyncTask base class calls this to obtain the new time-stamp value.

properties.name: Tasks
properties.summary: The complete collection of all of the currently scheduled tasks

types.name: TaskProcessMode
types.summary: Describes the way a scheduled task will be processed.

enum_values.name: Asynchronous
enum_values.summary: Scheduled task will be processed by schedule

enum_values.name: Synchronous
enum_values.summary: Scheduled task will be processed immediately

types.name: NotifyPropertyChangedInvocatorAttribute
types.summary: Indicates that the method is contained in a type that implements System.ComponentModel.INotifyPropertyChanged interface and this method is used to notify that some property value changed.
types.remarks: The method should be non-static and conform to one of the supported signatures: ; NotifyChanged(string); NotifyChanged(params string[]); NotifyChanged{T}(Expression{Func{T}}); NotifyChanged{T,U}(Expression{Func{T,U}}); SetProperty{T}(ref T, T, string)

properties.name: ParameterName
properties.summary: NotifyPropertyChangedInvocatorAttribute.ParameterName

types.name: MigrationManager
types.summary: This class contains methods and properties used for migration of Dynamo workspaces.

constructors.name: MigrationManager(Dynamo.Migration.MigrationManager.FutureFileCallback, Dynamo.Migration.MigrationManager.ObsoleteFileCallback)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of Dynamo.Migration.MigrationManager class

params.name: displayFutureFileMessage
params.doc: Functions that can be used as a callback in the event a file from a later version of Dynamo is attempting to be loaded

params.name: displayObsoleteFileMessage
params.doc: Functions that can be used as a callback in the event an obsolete file is attempted to be loaded.

methods.name: AddMigrationType(Dynamo.Graph.Nodes.TypeLoadData)
methods.summary: Adds a new type containing Migration methods into this manager.

params.name: t
params.doc: Type data to add

methods.name: CloneAndChangeName(System.Xml.XmlElement, string, string, bool)
methods.signature: System.Xml.XmlElement CloneAndChangeName(System.Xml.XmlElement element, string type, string name, bool cloneInnerXml)
methods.summary: Call this method to create a clone of the original XmlElement and change its type at one go. This method preserves all the attributes while updating only the type name.

params.name: element
params.doc: The XmlElement to be cloned and the type name updated.

params.name: type
params.doc: The fully qualified name of the new type.

params.name: name
params.doc: The new name, by which this node is known.

params.name: cloneInnerXml
params.doc: Parameter indicating whether the inner xml of the original node should be cloned.

returns.doc: Returns the cloned and updated XmlElement.

methods.name: CreateCodeBlockNodeFrom(System.Xml.XmlElement)
methods.summary: Call this method to create an empty Code Block node, with all attributes carried over from an existing src XmlElement.

params.name: srcElement
params.doc: The source element from which the Code Block node XmlElement is constructed. All attributes of the source XmlElement will be copied over, and Code Block node specific attributes will be added.

returns.doc: Returns an XmlElement that represents the resulting Code Block node.

methods.name: CreateCodeBlockNodeModelNode(System.Xml.XmlDocument, System.Xml.XmlElement, int, string)
methods.signature: System.Xml.XmlElement CreateCodeBlockNodeModelNode(System.Xml.XmlDocument document, System.Xml.XmlElement oldNode, int nodeIndex, string codeText)
methods.summary: This method creates an empty CodeBlockNodeModel node that contains basic code block node information.

params.name: document
params.doc: The XmlDocument to create the node in.

params.name: oldNode
params.doc: Base node to create a new one

params.name: nodeIndex
params.doc: Index of the node

params.name: codeText
params.doc: Code block content

returns.doc: Returns the XmlElement that represents a CodeBlockNodeModel node with its basic function information with default attributes.

methods.name: CreateCustomNodeFrom(System.Xml.XmlDocument, System.Xml.XmlElement, string, string, string, System.Collections.Generic.List<string>, System.Collections.Generic.List<string>)
methods.signature: System.Xml.XmlElement CreateCustomNodeFrom(System.Xml.XmlDocument document, System.Xml.XmlElement srcElement, string id, string name, string description, System.Collections.Generic.List<string> inputs, System.Collections.Generic.List<string> outputs)
methods.summary: Create a custom node as a replacement for an existing node.

params.name: document
params.doc: The XmlDocument to create the node in.

params.name: srcElement
params.doc: The source XmlElement object.

params.name: id
params.doc: The custom node id.

params.name: name
params.doc: The custom node name.

params.name: description
params.doc: The custom node's description.

params.name: inputs
params.doc: A list of input names.

params.name: outputs
params.doc: A list of output names.

returns.doc: Returns the XmlElement that represents a custom node with its basic function information with default attributes.

methods.name: CreateDummyNode(System.Xml.XmlElement, int, int)
methods.signature: System.Xml.XmlElement CreateDummyNode(System.Xml.XmlElement element, int inportCount, int outportCount)
methods.summary: Call this method to create a dummy node, should a node failed to be migrated. This results in a dummy node with a description of what the original node type was, and also retain the number of input and output ports.

params.name: element
params.doc: XmlElement representing the original node which has failed migration.

params.name: inportCount
params.doc: The number of input ports required on the new dummy node. This number must be a positive number greater or equal to zero.

params.name: outportCount
params.doc: The number of output ports required on the new dummy node. This number must be a positive number greater or equal to zero.

returns.doc: Returns a new XmlElement representing the dummy node.

methods.name: CreateFunctionNode(System.Xml.XmlDocument, System.Xml.XmlElement, int, string, string, string)
methods.signature: System.Xml.XmlElement CreateFunctionNode(System.Xml.XmlDocument document, System.Xml.XmlElement oldNode, int nodeIndex, string assembly, string name, string signature)
methods.summary: Call this method to create an empty DSFunction node that contains basic function node information.

params.name: document
params.doc: The XmlDocument to create the node in.

params.name: oldNode
params.doc: Base node to create a new one

params.name: nodeIndex
params.doc: Index of the node

params.name: assembly
params.doc: Name of the assembly that implements this function.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name to display on the node.

params.name: signature
params.doc: The signature of the function.

returns.doc: Returns the XmlElement that represents a DSFunction node with its basic function information with default attributes.

methods.name: CreateFunctionNodeFrom(System.Xml.XmlElement)
methods.summary: Call this method to create a duplicated XmlElement with all the attributes found from the source XmlElement.

params.name: srcElement
params.doc: The source XmlElement to duplicate.

returns.doc: Returns the duplicated XmlElement with all attributes found in the source XmlElement. The resulting XmlElement will also have a mandatory "type" attribute with value "Dynamo.Graph.Nodes.ZeroTouch.DSFunction".

methods.name: CreateFunctionNodeFrom(System.Xml.XmlElement, string[])
methods.summary: Call this method to create a XmlElement with a set of attributes carried over from the source XmlElement. The new XmlElement will have a name of "Dynamo.Graph.Nodes.ZeroTouch.DSFunction".

params.name: srcElement
params.doc: The source XmlElement object.

params.name: attribNames
params.doc: The list of attribute names whose values are to be carried over to the resulting XmlElement. This list is mandatory and it cannot be empty. If a specified attribute cannot be found in srcElement, an empty attribute with the same name will be created in the resulting XmlElement.

returns.doc: Returns the resulting XmlElement with specified attributes duplicated from srcElement. The resulting XmlElement will also have a mandatory "type" attribute with value "Dynamo.Graph.Nodes.ZeroTouch.DSFunction".

methods.name: CreateMissingNode(System.Xml.XmlElement, int, int)
methods.signature: System.Xml.XmlElement CreateMissingNode(System.Xml.XmlElement element, int inportCount, int outportCount)
methods.summary: Call this method to create a dummy node, should a node failed to be migrated. This results in a dummy node with a description of what the original node type was, and also retain the number of input and output ports.

params.name: element
params.doc: XmlElement representing the original node which has failed migration.

params.name: inportCount
params.doc: The number of input ports required on the new dummy node. This number must be a positive number greater or equal to zero.

params.name: outportCount
params.doc: The number of output ports required on the new dummy node. This number must be a positive number greater or equal to zero.

returns.doc: Returns a new XmlElement representing the dummy node.

methods.name: CreateNode(System.Xml.XmlDocument, System.Xml.XmlElement, int, string, string)
methods.signature: System.Xml.XmlElement CreateNode(System.Xml.XmlDocument document, System.Xml.XmlElement oldNode, int nodeIndex, string type, string name)
methods.summary: This method creates an empty NodeModel node that contains basic code block node information.

params.name: document
params.doc: The XmlDocument to create the node in.

params.name: oldNode
params.doc: Base node to create a new one

params.name: nodeIndex
params.doc: Index of the node

params.name: type
params.doc: Node type name

params.name: name
params.doc: Name to display on the node

returns.doc: Returns the XmlElement that represents a NodeModel node with its basic function information with default attributes.

methods.name: CreateVarArgFunctionNode(System.Xml.XmlDocument, System.Xml.XmlElement, int, string, string, string, string)
methods.signature: System.Xml.XmlElement CreateVarArgFunctionNode(System.Xml.XmlDocument document, System.Xml.XmlElement oldNode, int nodeIndex, string assembly, string name, string signature, string inputcount)

types.summary: Represents the information about the data type that needs to be included under a given LayoutElement.

properties.summary: Relative or absolute URL of the icon for the corresponding library item

properties.name: inclusive
properties.summary: Checks if a new category to be created for its path. If the include path is "A.B.C" under an element say "Root" and for two nodes fully qualified name is "A.B.C.D" and "A.B.C.E", then by default inclusive is true that means there we will get a category "C" under "Root" element as parent and "D" and "E" as children. If inclusive is false then both "D" and "E" will be direct children of "Root" and there won't be any intermediate element "C".

properties.name: path
properties.summary: Partial or full namespace to be included under a given layout element

types.name: LayoutElementType
types.summary: Represents the type of the element. Possible values are section, category, group, create, action, query and none.

enum_values.name: action
enum_values.summary: Elements of this type result in library items that get clubbed under the Action cluster

enum_values.name: category
enum_values.summary: Category elements represent the root library items contained in section elements.

enum_values.name: classType
enum_values.summary: classType comes directly under its parent category, it contains just text without icon

enum_values.name: create
enum_values.summary: Elements of this type result in library items that get clubbed under the Create cluster

enum_values.name: group
enum_values.summary: Groups comes directly under its parent category, it contains just text without icon

enum_values.name: none
enum_values.summary: All other expandable library items that are not categories or groups

enum_values.name: query
enum_values.summary: Elements of this type result in library items that get clubbed under the Query cluster

enum_values.name: section
enum_values.summary: Section elements represent the root items on the library view

params.name: text
params.doc: Text value of the element

properties.summary: List of data types that should be included under this given library element

events.summary: This event is raised right before the shutdown of DynamoModel started. When this event is raised, the shutdown is guaranteed to take place (i.e. user has had a chance to save the work and decided to proceed with shutting down Dynamo). Handlers of this event can still safely access the DynamoModel, the WorkspaceModel (along with its contents), and the DynamoScheduler.

types.name: IPathResolver
types.summary: This interface provides paths to external assemblies and node directories.

methods.name: GetDynamoUserDataLocations
methods.summary: Returns a list of user data folders on this system.

returns.doc: The implementation of this interface method should return a list of user data folders, one for each of Dynamo product installed on the system. When there is no Dynamo product installed, this method returns an empty list.

properties.name: AdditionalNodeDirectories
properties.summary: Additional directories in which node assemblies can be located. The return value of this property should never be null. Each entry must represent a valid directory, otherwise DirectoryNotFoundException exception is thrown.

properties.name: AdditionalResolutionPaths
properties.summary: Additional directories that should be considered when path resolution is done for a library that does not contain full path information. The return value of this property should never be null. Each entry must represent a valid directory, otherwise DirectoryNotFoundException exception is thrown.

properties.name: CommonDataRootFolder
properties.summary: This property represents the root folder where application common data files (i.e. shared among all users on the same machine) are stored. If this property returns a null or empty string, then PathManager falls back to using "%AppData%\Dynamo\Dynamo Core". If this property returns a string that does not represent an existing folder, PathManager will attempt to create a new directory. If the property does not represent a valid path string, an exception will be thrown by the underlying system IO API invoked. Note that this path should not include the version number as it will be appended by PathManager.

properties.name: PreloadedLibraryPaths
properties.summary: Libraries to be preloaded as part of Dynamo start up sequence. Each entry in this list can either represent full path to a library, or just the assembly name. If absolute path information is not supplied, the library will be looked up through both predefined and additional resolution paths. The return value of this property should never be null.

properties.name: UserDataRootFolder
properties.summary: This property represents the root folder where user specific data files are stored. If this property returns a null or empty string, then PathManager falls back to using "%ProgramData%\Dynamo\Dynamo Core". If this property returns a string that does not represent an existing folder, PathManager will attempt to create a new directory. If the property does not represent a valid path string, an exception will be thrown by the underlying system IO API invoked. Note that this path should not include the version number as it will be appended by PathManager.

types.name: IPathManager
types.summary: This interface provides the most common paths. E.g. core directory, package directory etc.

methods.name: AddResolutionPath(string)
methods.summary: Call this method to add additional path for consideration when path resolution take place.

params.name: path
params.doc: The full path to be considered when PathManager attempts to resolve a file path. If this argument does not represent a valid directory path, this method throws an exception.

methods.name: ResolveDocumentPath(ref string)
methods.summary: Given an initial RTF document file name, this method returns the absolute path of the file, if one exists.

params.name: document
params.doc: The name of the RTF file. This argument cannot be null or empty.

returns.doc: Returns true if the requested document can be located, or false otherwise.

methods.name: ResolveLibraryPath(ref string)
methods.summary: Given an initial file path with the file name, resolve the full path to the target file.

params.name: library
params.doc: The initial library file path. This argument can optionally include the full path with a target file name. If a full path is given and it represents an invalid file path, the file name will be searched for in additional resolution paths.

returns.doc: Returns true if the requested file can be located, or false otherwise.

properties.name: CommonDefinitions

properties.name: DefinitionDirectories
properties.summary: Directories from where custom nodes are to be loaded. The implementor of this interface method should always guarantee that a non-empty list is returned, and that the first entry represents the default custom node directory. Custom nodes created are stored in the default directory, which is specific to the current user. Changes to custom nodes may or may not be saved to their current location depending on write access.

properties.summary: Directories from where packages are to be loaded. The implementor of this interface method should always guarantee that a non-empty list is returned, and that the first entry represents the default package directory. Packages downloaded through package manager are stored in the default package directory, which is specific to the current user.

properties.name: UserDataDirectory
properties.summary: The local directory that contains user specific data files.

methods.name: UpdateValueCore(Dynamo.Graph.UpdateValueParams)
methods.summary: This method is supplied as a generic way for command framework to update a given named-value in a ModelBase (which has to work under both user and playback scenarios). During playback, the command framework issues pre-recorded UpdateModelValueCommand that targets a model. Since there is no data-binding at play here, there will not be IValueConverter. This method takes only string input (the way they appear in DynamoTextBox), which overridden method can use for value conversion.

methods.signature: bool RequestVisualUpdateAsync(Dynamo.Scheduler.IScheduler scheduler, Dynamo.Engine.EngineController engine, Dynamo.Visualization.IRenderPackageFactory factory, bool forceUpdate)
methods.summary: Call this method to asynchronously regenerate render package for this node. This method accesses core properties of a NodeModel and therefore is typically called on the main/UI thread.

params.name: scheduler
params.doc: An IScheduler on which the task will be scheduled.

params.name: engine
params.doc: The EngineController which will be used to get MirrorData for the node.

params.name: factory
params.doc: An IRenderPackageFactory which will be used to generate IRenderPackage objects.

params.name: forceUpdate
params.doc: Normally, render packages are only generated when the node's IsUpdated parameter is true. By setting forceUpdate to true, the render packages will be updated.

returns.doc: Flag which indicates if geometry update has been scheduled

Output nodes can be any node that returns a single output or a dictionary. Code block nodes and Custom nodes are specifically excluded at this time even though they can return a single output for sake of clarity.

properties.summary: Checks if this group contains any nested groups.

properties.name: HasToggledUnconnectedOutPorts
properties.summary: Indicates whether the user manually toggled the visibility of unconnected output ports. If true, this overrides the global preference setting.

properties.summary: Returns the full height of the group. That means ModelAreaHeight + TextBlockHeight

properties.summary: Adjustment margin to be added to the height of the group. When set the height of the group will always be set to Height + heightAdjustment

properties.summary: Required to calculate the TOP position in a group

properties.summary: Adjustment margin to be added to the width of the group. When set the width of the group will always be set to Width + widthAdjustment

enum_values.summary: Both the AsyncTask objects in comparison should be kept.

enum_values.summary: The current instance of AsyncTask should be discarded while keeping the other AsyncTask in comparison.

enum_values.summary: The current instance of AsyncTask should be kept while discarding the other AsyncTask in comparison.

enum_values.name: CompletionHandled
enum_values.summary: Post-execute action of task is completed and registered event handlers of task completion are notified.

enum_values.name: Discarded
enum_values.summary: Task is dropped from the scheduler due to compacting process

enum_values.name: ExecutionCompleted
enum_values.summary: Task execution is completed successfully

enum_values.name: ExecutionFailed
enum_values.summary: Task execution is completed with errors

enum_values.name: ExecutionStarting
enum_values.summary: Task is about to be executed

enum_values.name: Scheduled
enum_values.summary: Task is added to the scheduler

enum_values.name: Abort
enum_values.summary: The migration should not proceed and the file open operation should be aborted. This can be used to indicate that a version of file that is no longer supported and no migration path is provided.

enum_values.name: Migrate
enum_values.summary: File migration should proceed to migrate the older file version to a newer one.

enum_values.name: Retain
enum_values.summary: The file version is up-to-date and the file can be used as-is without migration.

params.name: tag
params.doc: A string tag to be assigned to the command. This parameter can be any string, even an empty one. However, it should not be null. A null "tag" parameter causes the ArgumentNullException to be thrown.

methods.name: ExecuteCore(Dynamo.Models.DynamoModel)
methods.summary: Derived classes must implement this method to perform the actual command execution. A typical implementation of this method involves calling a corresponding method on DynamoModel by passing itself as the only argument.

params.name: dynamoModel
params.doc: The DynamoModel object on which this command should be executed.

methods.name: SerializeCore(System.Xml.XmlElement)
methods.summary: Derived classes must implement this method to serialize all relevant information into the XmlElement supplied to it. Typically the method is a direct mirror of DeserializeCore.

params.name: element
params.doc: All arguments that are required for this command are written into this XmlElement. The information written here must be exactly what DeserializeCore method expects.

properties.name: CannotRemovePackageAssemblyMessage
properties.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to Due to limitations in the .NET framework, it is not possible to update your package assembly while it is already loaded. Please update the assembly while Dynamo is not running and try again..

properties.name: CannotRemovePackageAssemblyTitle
properties.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to Cannot update assembly.

properties.name: Culture
properties.summary: Overrides the current thread's CurrentUICulture property for all resource lookups using this strongly typed resource class.

properties.name: DownloadPackageDisabled
properties.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to Package download is disabled in no-network mode..

properties.name: InvalidPackageFolderWarning
properties.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to The folder '{0}' does not exist.

properties.name: InvalidPackageMalformedNodeLibraryDefinition
properties.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to "A package called {0} found at {1} has improperly defined its node libraries in the package manifest. Ignoring it.".

properties.name: InvalidPackageNodeLibraryIsNotSigned
properties.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to "A package called {0} found at {1} could not be verified to have signed dll files. {2} Ignoring it.".

properties.name: PackagesDirectorySkipped
properties.summary: Looks up a localized string similar to {0} was not scanned for packages because a preference setting disabled loading from that location.".

methods.name: LoadCustomNodesAndPackages(Dynamo.PackageManager.LoadPackageParams, Dynamo.Core.CustomNodeManager)
methods.summary: LoadCustomNodesAndPackages can be used to load custom nodes and packages from temporary paths that do not need to be added to preference settings. To load from temporary custom paths, initialize a local PreferenceSettings object and add the paths to its CustomPackageFolders property, then initialize a new LoadPackageParams with this preferences object and use as input to this method. To load from custom paths that need to be persisted to the preferences, initialize a LoadPackageParams from an existing preferences object.

events.name: PackgeLoaded

enum_values.name: Error
enum_values.summary: The package was not loaded in Dynamo because of an error. See the Error property for more information

enum_values.name: Loaded
enum_values.summary: The package is fully loaded and is ready to be used

enum_values.name: None
enum_values.summary: Invalid state. The initial state for every package, before it is interpreted by Dynamo

enum_values.name: Unloaded
enum_values.summary: The package is not loaded in Dynamo and not deleted from package locations

enum_values.name: None
enum_values.summary: Invalid scheduled state. The initial state for every package, before it is interpreted by Dynamo

enum_values.name: ScheduledForDeletion
enum_values.summary: The package is scheduled to be deleted. After the next Dynamo restart, the package will deleted from the package locations

enum_values.name: ScheduledForUnload
enum_values.summary: The package is scheduled to be unloaded. After the next Dynamo restart, the package will be in an Unloaded state

types.name: Preloader
types.summary: Shape manager preloader class that helps with preloading Autodesk Shape Manager (ASM) binaries through geometry library (LibG). This class being part of Dynamo core module, relies on IGeometryConfiguration supplied by the host application to determine the installed location of ASM binaries.

constructors.name: Preloader(string)
constructors.summary: Constructs a preloader object to help preload shape manager.

params.name: rootFolder
params.doc: Full path of the directory that contains LibG_xxx folder, where 'xxx' represents the library version. In a typical setup this would be the same directory that contains Dynamo core modules. This must represent a valid directory.

constructors.name: Preloader(string, System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<System.Version>)
constructors.summary: Constructs a preloader object to help preload a specific version of shape manager.

params.name: rootFolder
params.doc: Full path of the directory that contains LibG_major_minor_build folder. In a typical setup this would be the same directory that contains Dynamo core modules. This must represent a valid directory.

params.name: versions
params.doc: A list of version numbers to check for in order of preference. This argument cannot be null or empty.

methods.name: Preload
methods.summary: Attempts to load the geometry library binaries using the version and location specified when the Preloader was constructed.

properties.name: GeometryFactoryPath
properties.summary: Preloader.GeometryFactoryPath

properties.name: PreloaderLocation
properties.summary: Preloader.PreloaderLocation

properties.name: ShapeManagerPath
properties.summary: Preloader.ShapeManagerPath

properties.name: Version2
properties.summary: Preloader.Version2

methods.name: GetGeometryFactoryPath2(string, System.Version)
methods.summary: This method will return the path to the GeometryFactory assembly location for a requested version of the geometry library. This method is tolerant to the requested version in that it will attempt to locate an exact or lower version of the GeometryFactory assembly.

params.name: rootFolder
params.doc: Full path of the directory that contains LibG_xxx_y_z folder, where 'xxx y z' represents the library version of asm. In a typical setup this would be the same directory that contains Dynamo core modules. This must represent a valid directory - it cannot be null.

params.name: version
params.doc: Version number of the targeted geometry library. If the resulting assembly does not exist, this method will look for a lower version match. This parameter cannot be null.

returns.doc: The full path to GeometryFactoryAssembly assembly.

methods.name: GetInstalledAsmVersion2(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<System.Version>, ref string, string, System.Func<string, System.Collections.IEnumerable>)
methods.signature: System.Version GetInstalledAsmVersion2(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<System.Version> versions, ref string location, string rootFolder, System.Func<string, System.Collections.IEnumerable> getASMInstallsFunc)
methods.summary: Call this method to determine the version of ASM that is installed on the user machine. The method scans through a list of known Autodesk product folders for ASM binaries with the targeted version.

params.name: versions
params.doc: A IEnumerable of version numbers to check for in order of preference. This argument cannot be null or empty.

params.name: location
params.doc: The full path of the directory in which targeted ASM binaries are found. This argument cannot be null.

params.name: rootFolder
params.doc: This method makes use of DynamoInstallDetective to determine the installation location of various Autodesk products. This argument is not optional and must represent the full path to the folder which contains DynamoInstallDetective.dll. An exception is thrown if the assembly cannot be located.

params.name: getASMInstallsFunc
params.doc: A delegate which can be used to replace the default ASM install lookup method. This is primarily used for testing. The delegate should return an IEnumerable of Tuples - these represent versions of ASM which are located on the user's machine.

returns.doc: Returns System.Version of ASM if any installed ASM is found, or null otherwise.

methods.name: GetLibGPreloaderLocation(System.Version, string)
methods.summary: Get the corresponding libG preloader location for the target ASM loading version. If there is exact match preloader version to the target ASM version, use it, otherwise use the closest below.

params.name: version
params.doc: The target loading version of ASM.

params.name: rootFolder
params.doc: Full path of the directory that contains LibG_major_minor_build folder. In a typical setup this would be the same directory that contains Dynamo core modules. This must represent a valid directory.

methods.name: GetVersionFromPath(string, string)
methods.summary: Extracts version of ASM dlls from a path by scanning for ASM dlls in the path. Throws if ASM binaries cannot be found in the path.

params.name: asmPath
params.doc: path to directory containing asm dlls

params.name: searchPattern
params.doc: optional - to be used for testing - default is the ASM search pattern.

methods.name: PreloadAsmFromPath(string, string)
methods.summary: Call this method to preload ASM binaries from a specific location. This method does not have a return value, any failures in loading ASM binaries will result in an exception being thrown.

params.name: preloaderLocation
params.doc: Full path of the folder that contains PreloaderAssembly assembly. This argument must represent a valid path to the loader.

params.name: asmLocation
params.doc: Full path of the folder that contains ASM binaries to load. This argument cannot be null or empty.

methods.summary: Handler to the ApplicationDomain's AssemblyResolve event. If an assembly's location cannot be resolved, an exception is thrown. types.name: ReferenceEqualityComparer
types.summary: This class compares two CLR objects using reference equality. It is used in CLRObjectMap to map CLR object instances to their marshaled StackValue representations. Uses System.Runtime.CompilerServices.RuntimeHelpers.GetHashCode(System.Object) to get the object's identity hash code, which ensures well-distributed hash codes even when objects have value-based hash codes that collide (e.g., Point objects with identical coordinates). Note: The hash code computation is intentionally aligned with the reference equality behavior used by System.Object.ReferenceEquals(System.Object,System.Object). This ensures consistent semantics between equality comparison and hash code generation, which is a requirement for proper System.Collections.Generic.IEqualityComparer`1 implementation.

types.name: RuntimeCore
types.summary: RuntimeCore is an object that is instantiated once across the lifecycle of the runtime This is the entry point of the runtime VM and its input is a DS Executable format. There will only be one instance of RuntimeCore regardless of how many times instances of a DSASM.Executive (runtime VM) is instantiated. Its properties will be persistent and accessible across all instances of a DSASM.Executive

types.name: RuntimeData
types.summary: The RuntimeData is an object that contains properties that is consumed only by the runtime VM It is instantiated prior to execution and is populated with information gathered from the CompileCore The runtime VM is designed to run independently from the front-end (UI, compiler) and the only 2 properties it needs are the RuntimeData and the DSExecutable. The RuntimeData will also contain properties that are populated at runtime and consumed at runtime.

methods.name: GetZippedIndices(System.Collections.Generic.List<ProtoCore.DSASM.StackValue>, ProtoCore.RuntimeCore)
methods.summary: For an array we supporting zipped replicaiton for array indexing as well. I.e., for the following expression: a[1..3][2..4] = x; It will be expanded to a[1][2] = x; a[2][3] = x; a[3][4] = x; So here we need to calculate zipped indices. The length of returned indices is decided by the shortest length of index that used in array indexing. E.g., For array indexing [{1, 2, 3}][{"x", "y"}][{6, 7, 8}], i.e., 1 -> "x" -> 6 2 -> "y" -> 7 3 -> -> 8 The shortest length of index is 2 ({"x", "y"}), so function will returns: {{1, "x", 6}, {2, "y", 7}}

methods.summary: Retrieves the string format of the identifier list from left to right, leaving out any symbols after the last identifier. Given: A.B() Return: "A" Given: A.B.C()[0] Return: "A.B" Given: A.B().C Return: "A" Given: A.B[0].C Return: "A.B[0].C" Given: A().B (global function) Return: empty string Given: A.B[0].C() Return: "A.B[0]"

types.name: MirrorObject
types.summary: An abstract MirrorObject that represents a generic DesignScript object that can reflected Reflection on this object can be done at compiletime or runtime

types.name: RuntimeMirror
types.summary: A RuntimeMirror object is used to reflect on the runtime status of a single designsript variable

types.name: StaticMirror

methods.name: GetInstanceMembers

returns.doc: symbol nodes

methods.name: Permute(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<T>, int)
methods.summary: Returns an enumeration of enumerators, one for each permutation of the input. E.g. given an input array: {A, B, C, D, E}, and count of 2, this returns the following permutations in the same order: { AB, AC, AD, AE, BC, BD, BE, CD, CE, DE }

params.name: list
params.doc: input collection to choose permutations from

params.name: count
params.doc: Number of items to return permutations of

returns.doc: All possible permuatations of the input list picking (count) items at a time

types.name: Symbol
types.summary: Symbol class : It represents a symbol with namespace.

constructors.name: Symbol(string)
constructors.summary: Constructs a FullyQualifiedSymbolName with the given fullname.

params.name: fullname
params.doc: fullname for the symbol including namespaces.

methods.name: GetShortestUniqueNames(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable<ProtoCore.Namespace.Symbol>)
methods.summary: Given a list of conflicting namespaces, finds the shortest partial name for a namespace that can be resolved uniquely. For example, given {"A.B.C.D.E", "X.Y.A.B.E.C.E", "X.Y.A.C.B.E"}, all with the same class E, their shortest unique names would be: {"D.E", "E.E", "C.B.E"}

params.name: symbolList
params.doc: Input list of conflicting namespaces (having same class name)

returns.doc: Map of Symbol vs. short name

methods.name: Matches(string)
methods.summary: Checks if all of the namespace prefixes specified in the given partially qualified name appear in this namespace in the same order. For Example: A full namespace "Com.Autodesk.Designscript.ProtoGeometry.Point" will match all of the following partial namespaces Com.Autodesk.Designscript.ProtoGeometry.Point Point DesignScript.Point ProtoGeometry.Point Autodesk.DesignScript.Point whereas it won't match Com.DesignScript.Autodesk.Point

params.name: partialname
params.doc: Partially qualified symbol name

returns.doc: returns true if partial name matches this

methods.summary: Equality operator overload to have same equality behaviour as object.Equals() override

methods.name: op_Inequality(ProtoCore.Namespace.Symbol, ProtoCore.Namespace.Symbol)
methods.summary: Inequality operator overload is needed for every equality operator overload

properties.summary: True iff execution is complete

properties.name: InitialDepth
properties.summary: The depth of the replicating member function used in determining thisPtr

properties.summary: These are cached at the first replicating call so that they can be used in GC when the replication is Done

properties.name: InitialPC
properties.summary: The instruction point of the replicating CALLR instruction needs to be cached in order to return to the next instruction at end of replication

properties.name: IsFirstCall
properties.summary: This flag indicates whether the current FEP call is a continuation of a replicating call

properties.name: NextDispatchArgs
properties.summary: This is the resolved list of arguments that should be used to execute the function end point

properties.name: Result
properties.summary: This represents the result. If execution of the callsite is complete this is the retun value, otherwise it is a partial value

methods.signature: ProtoCore.DSASM.StackValue RestoreDominantStructure(ProtoCore.DSASM.StackValue ret, ProtoCore.Lang.Replication.DominantListStructure domStructure, System.Collections.Generic.List<ProtoCore.Lang.Replication.ReplicationInstruction> instructions, ProtoCore.RuntimeCore runtimeCore)
methods.summary: If an input is a dominant list, restructure the result based on the structure of dominant list. Note the dominant structure will be restored only if the dominant list is zipped with other arguments, or the replication is applied to the dominant list firstly.

types.name: ZipAlgorithm
types.summary: How should the zip algorithm handle cases where one data stream is longer than the other

params.name: count
params.doc: Count is the number of elements left for allocation

params.name: maxAlloc
params.doc: Max alloc is the maximum potential reductions that can yet be applied

methods.name: BuildPartialReplicationInstructions(System.Collections.Generic.List<System.Collections.Generic.List<ProtoCore.ReplicationGuide>>)
methods.summary: Calculate partial replication instruciton based on replication guide level. For example, for foo(xs<1><2><3>, ys<1><1><2>, zs<1><1><3>), the guides are: level | 0 | 1 | 2 | ------+-----+-----+-----+ xs | 1 | 2 | 3 | ------+-----+-----+-----+ ys | 1 | 1 | 2 | ------+-----+-----+-----+ zs | 1 | 1 | 3 | This function goes through each level and calculate replication instructions. replication instructions on level 0: Zip replication on (0, 1, 2) (i.e., zip on xs, ys, zs) replication instructions on level 1: Zip replication on (1, 2) (i.e., zip on ys, zs) Cartesian replication on 0 (i.e., on xs) replication instructions on level 2: Cartesian replication on 1 (i.e., on ys) Zip replication on (0, 2) (i.e., zip on xs, zs)

methods.name: BuildReplicationCombinations(System.Collections.Generic.List<ProtoCore.Lang.Replication.ReplicationInstruction>, System.Collections.Generic.List<ProtoCore.DSASM.StackValue>, ProtoCore.RuntimeCore)
methods.summary: Build all possible replications based on the rank of parameters and the provided replicatoin guide.

methods.name: ComputeAllReducedParams(System.Collections.Generic.List<ProtoCore.DSASM.StackValue>, System.Collections.Generic.List<ProtoCore.Lang.Replication.ReplicationInstruction>, ProtoCore.RuntimeCore)
methods.summary: For each parameter, if there is a replication instruction for it, and if it is an array, expand parameter list based on the types of elements in that array. For example, for parameters {p1, p2, ..., pk, ..., pn} where pk is an array {a1:int, a2:string, a3:double, ...} and there is a Cartesian replication on pk, the parameter list will be expanded to {p1, p2, ..., a1, ..., pn} {p1, p2, ..., a2, ..., pn} {p1, p2, ..., a3, ..., pn} ...

methods.name: EstimateReducedParams(System.Collections.Generic.List<ProtoCore.DSASM.StackValue>, System.Collections.Generic.List<ProtoCore.Lang.Replication.ReplicationInstruction>, ProtoCore.RuntimeCore)
methods.summary: Compute the effects of the replication guides on the formal parameter lists The results of this loose data, and will not be correct on jagged arrays of hetrogenius types

methods.name: GetMaxReductionDepth(ProtoCore.DSASM.StackValue, ProtoCore.RuntimeCore)
methods.summary: Returns the maximum depth to which an element can be reduced This will include cases where only partial reduction can be performed on jagged arrays

methods.name: ReductionToInstructions(System.Collections.Generic.List<int>, System.Collections.Generic.List<ProtoCore.Lang.Replication.ReplicationInstruction>)
methods.summary: Convert reduction to instruction. Using zip-first strategy. For example, 0 2 4 > Zip on 1,2 0 1 3 > Zip on 1,2 0 0 2 > Cartesian on 2 0 0 1 > Cartesian on 2

types.name: DynamicFunction
types.summary: It represents an unresolved function in the code. For any unresolved function, a DynamicFunction instance will be created and be added to DynamicFunctionTable. At runtime, callr will fetch the corresponding DynamicFunction instance from DynamicFunctionTable, and based on its name/argument number/class scope to resolves function dynamically.

types.name: BiDictionaryOneToOne<TFirst, TSecond>
types.summary: This is a dictionary guaranteed to have only one of each value and key. It may be searched either by TFirst or by TSecond, giving a unique answer because it is 1 to 1.

methods.summary: Tries to add the pair to the dictionary. Returns false if either element is already in the dictionary

returns.doc: true if successfully added, false if either element are already in the dictionary

methods.name: TryGetByFirst(TFirst, ref TSecond)
methods.signature: bool TryGetByFirst(TFirst first, ref TSecond second)
methods.summary: Find the TSecond corresponding to the TFirst first. Returns false if first is not in the dictionary.

params.name: first
params.doc: the key to search for

params.name: second
params.doc: the corresponding value

returns.doc: true if first is in the dictionary, false otherwise

methods.name: TryGetBySecond(TSecond, ref TFirst)
methods.signature: bool TryGetBySecond(TSecond second, ref TFirst first)

params.name: second
params.doc: the key to search for

params.name: first
params.doc: the corresponding value

returns.doc: true if second is in the dictionary, false otherwise

types.name: DataBridge
types.summary: Provides callback registration by GUID, allows for hooking Actions into the VM.

methods.name: BridgeData(string, object)
methods.summary: Calls the registered callback for the given guid string with the given data. This is safe to include in standalone DS scripts, since if there are no callbacks registered then the method will do nothing.

params.name: id
params.doc: String identifying which registered callback to invoke.

params.name: data
params.doc: Data to be passed to the callback.

methods.name: GenerateBridgeDataAst(string, ProtoCore.AST.AssociativeAST.AssociativeNode)
methods.summary: Produces AST that, when executed by the VM, will perform Data Bridging by calling BridgeData.

params.name: id
params.doc: Guid identifying which registered callback to be invoked.

params.name: input
params.doc: AST representing the data to be passed to the callback.

methods.name: RegisterCallback(string, System.Action<object>)
methods.summary: Registers a callback for a given GUID, to be invoked by the VM on an arbitrary thread. There are no guarantees as to what thread the callback will be invoked on.

params.name: id
params.doc: Guid used to identify the callback.

params.name: callback
params.doc: Action to be invoked with data from the VM.

methods.name: UnregisterCallback(string)
methods.summary: Unregisters a callback for a given GUID.

params.name: id
params.doc: Guid identifying the callback to be removed.
